rules: Controllers must only import services, decorators, and guards — never adapters, db, schema, or Drizzle operators
Services must use database adapters from src/adapters/ for all data access — never import db directly
Services must not import other services — use events, queues, or shared lib/graph layer for cross-service orchestration
Agents receive Database and Embedder via constructor injection — never import db or adapters directly
Graph factories accept dependencies (database, embedder, agents) via constructor — no hardcoded infrastructure dependencies
Graph factories must use Pick<Database, ...> for narrow interface contracts — never depend on full Database interface
Adapters must not import from @indexnetwork/protocol interfaces — define their own types that align with protocol interfaces, named by concept not technology
Queues orchestrate by calling services, graphs, or adapters — no business logic in queue handlers
Controller tests must use services only for setup, teardown, and assertions — never adapters, db, or schema
Protocol layer (@indexnetwork/protocol) must not depend on adapters or infrastructure — dependencies are injected via interfaces
Dependencies point inward: Controllers → Services → Adapters → Infrastructure; Protocol layer receives dependencies via injection
Events and queues are the only mechanism for cross-service communication

versioning: Bump backend/package.json version for changes under backend/
Bump frontend/package.json version for changes under frontend/
Bump packages/cli/package.json version for changes under packages/cli/

implementation: File naming: {domain}.{purpose}.ts (e.g. intent.inferrer.ts, chat.graph.ts) — no camelCase filenames
Adapter naming: use conceptual names (database.adapter.ts), never implementation names (drizzle.adapter.ts)
Import ordering: external packages → deep relative (../../+) → nearby relative (./ or ../)
Use Zod for all agent input/output schemas with .describe() on every field
Use createModel() from model.config.ts for all LLM configuration — never hardcode model names
Agents must be pure: no direct DB access, no side effects — services handle persistence
Every LangGraph graph must have at least one conditional edge and use Annotation.Root with reducers
Singleton pattern for services: export class + export const instance at bottom of file
Prefer soft deletes (deletedAt) over hard deletes
Snake_case for queue job names (e.g. generate_hyde, discover_opportunities)
Kebab-case for trace event agent names (e.g. intent-inferrer, profile-generator)
Emit trace events (graph_start/end, agent_start/end) around all graph invocations and agent calls
Migration files must be renamed to {NNNN}_{action}_{target}.sql with matching _journal.json tag
documentation: TSDoc on all classes and public methods (summary, @param, @returns, @throws)
delivery: Conventional commits (feat/fix/refactor/test/docs/chore) and conventional branch names (type/short-description)

Two remotes: origin (fork) and upstream (main repo). Push worktree branches to origin, create PRs from origin into upstream/dev. After merging a PR into upstream/dev, pull from upstream and push to origin to keep both in sync.

For npm-published subtree packages (`packages/cli/`, `packages/protocol/`), bump the base package version before promoting to main. Subtree dev pushes publish `-rc` prereleases under the `rc` npm tag; subtree main pushes publish the stable version when it is not already on npm.
triage: Always use worktrees for feature and fix branches — keep dev stable
Target specific test files (bun test path/to/file.spec.ts), never run full suite unnecessarily
